_meta.description: City of Montreal municipal permits for home-based businesses and commercial locations. Covers zoning compliance, occupancy permits, and borough-specific rules.

title_en: City of Montréal Municipal Permits & Licences
title_fr: Permis et licences municipaux — Ville de Montréal
plain_language_summary: Beyond provincial registration, Montréal has its own municipal requirements for operating a business. Home-based businesses have specific rules about what's allowed. Commercial businesses need an occupancy permit and possibly a business licence. Rules vary by borough and specific zoning.

home_based_business_rules.title: Home-Based Business (Travail à domicile / Entreprise à domicile)
home_based_business_rules.plain_language: Montréal allows many types of home-based businesses, but with restrictions to maintain residential character. Most micro-entrepreneurs starting out work from home legally — but there are limits.
generally_permitted: Office work, consulting, professional services done from home
Online sales with no customer traffic to your home
Crafts and small-scale production with no employees
Small food production under MAPAQ home kitchen exemption
generally_not_permitted_in_residential_z: Receiving regular customer traffic at your home
Visible signage on your home (beyond small identification plaque in some zones)
Employees working on-site at your home
Operating equipment that causes noise, odours, or traffic

how_to_verify_your_specific_zone.note: Enter your address to see what's permitted in your specific zone. Rules vary significantly by borough.

home_based_business_rules.borough_contact: Your borough office can clarify what's permitted for your specific situation. Contacts at: https://montreal.ca/en/topics/boroughs

permits_by_situation.situation: Home-based business with no client visits and no employees
permits_by_situation.note: Generally no permit needed. Confirm with your borough that your activity is permitted in your residential zone.

permits_by_situation.situation: Home-based business with occasional client visits
permits_by_situation.permit_required: Possibly — depends on zone and frequency
permits_by_situation.note: Contact your borough. Some zones explicitly permit occasional professional meetings at home.

permits_by_situation.situation: Commercial space — new tenant taking over existing commercial space
permits_by_situation.cost: Varies by project — typically $200–$500 for change of occupancy

permits_by_situation.situation: Commercial space — renovations or changes to the space
permits_by_situation.note: Required before any structural work. Apply to your borough.

permits_by_situation.situation: Food truck or mobile food business

permits_by_situation.note: Home daycares recognized by a Bureau Coordonnateur generally operate under provincial (MFA) authorization, not municipal permits. Confirm with your borough.

business_licence.required_for: Certain specific business types: restaurants, bars, certain retail, tattoo studios, massage establishments, etc.
business_licence.not_required_for: Most home-based businesses, professional services, freelancers, and general retail not on the licence list
business_licence.how_to_check: Use the Montreal business licence lookup or contact 311
business_licence.cost: Varies by business type — typically $100–$500/year if required

signage_rules.plain_language: If you have a commercial location, your signage must comply with Montréal's sign regulations AND Bill 96 French language requirements.
key_rules: French must be markedly predominant on all commercial signage
English text may appear alongside but must be noticeably smaller
Certain sign types require a permit from the borough
Illuminated signs, projecting signs, and roof signs typically require permits

prerequisites.step: Get your NEQ number from the REQ
prerequisites.required_for: Applying for commercial occupancy permits and business licences

cost_summary.note: Most home-based micro-entrepreneurs pay nothing to the city at startup

common_mistakes: Not checking zoning before signing a commercial lease — some zones don't permit your type of business
Assuming home-based is always fine — some business types (regular client traffic, employees on-site) violate residential zoning
Not getting an occupancy permit before opening a commercial space — the city can order you to close
Forgetting that signage requires its own permit in most commercial zones

contact.pmemtl: PME MTL can help navigate permits — https://www.pmemtl.com
